title: cerbera borbonica
intro: Cerbera borbonica, also known as the ocean apple or beach apple, is a toxic plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Australia, with its fruit being a potential source of food for various marine species. The plant has been found to contain cerberin, a toxic compound with medicinal properties that can be used to treat certain health conditions. Traditionally, the fruit of the Cerbera borbonica plant has been used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever reduction, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Cerbera borbonica include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory agent and its ability to support digestive health.
